description: 🪐 🔧 Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server for Jupyter.

findings.title: Arbitrary Code Execution: executes arbitrary code in Jupyter kernel
findings.description: jupyter-mcp-server allows direct execution of arbitrary Python/R/Julia code in a Jupyter kernel via execute_code and run_cell tools. This is the highest possible code execution risk — unrestricted kernel access with full OS-level capabilities from the kernel process.
findings.recommendation: BLOCK or require explicit approval for every invocation. Run the Jupyter kernel in a gVisor/sandbox container. Restrict kernel networking and filesystem access. Never expose to untrusted agents.

findings.title: Dangerous Permission: exec + filesystem + network permissions implied
findings.description: A Jupyter kernel has full exec, fs, and network capabilities by default. Any agent invoking this server can read/write files, make network requests, and execute OS commands via subprocess.
findings.recommendation: Use kernel gateway with restricted profiles. Apply strict network and filesystem policies at the container level.
